title: Apicra spiralis
intro: Apicra spiralis, also known as Mexican prickly poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Apicra spiralis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Apicra spiralis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Apicra spiralis is classified within the plant family Solanaceae. It belongs to the genus Apicra.
